family_members.industry_or_organization: Former leftist journalist; press secretary to PM Lee Kuan Yew

family_members.name: Liew Yuen Sien

political_stances.stance_content: Launched the Singapore Green Plan 2030, a whole-of-nation sustainability movement advancing Singapore's national agenda on sustainable development.
Expressed concern over planned 36-37% income cuts for ministers, saying further reductions would make it harder for anyone considering political office.
Recognised on the Forbes Sustainability Leaders List and TIME100 Climate List for contributions to addressing climate change and coastal protection.
Member of the Global Leaders Group on Antimicrobial Resistance, co-chaired by PM Sheikh Hasina of Bangladesh and PM Mia Mottley of Barbados.

major_achievements.achievement: Became the second woman in Singapore's history to become a full Cabinet minister

major_achievements.organization: Ministry of Culture, Community and Youth
major_achievements.achievement: Became the first female minister in Singapore to head a ministry

major_achievements.organization: Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment
major_achievements.achievement: Appointed Minister for Sustainability and the Environment, overseeing Singapore's climate and environmental policy
Elected President of the Singapore National Olympic Council
Recognised on Forbes Sustainability Leaders List and TIME100 Climate List

biography_summary: Grace Fu Hai Yien is a Singaporean accountant and politician who has served as Minister for Sustainability and the Environment since 2020 and Minister-in-charge of Trade Relations since 2024. Her father, James Fu, was press secretary to PM Lee Kuan Yew. Before politics, she had a career at Overseas Union Bank, Haw Par Group, and PSA Corporation (rising to CEO of PSA South East Asia and Japan). In 2012, she became the second woman to become a full Cabinet minister, and in 2015, the first woman to head a ministry. She is President of the Singapore National Olympic Council since 2024 and was recognised on the Forbes Sustainability Leaders List and TIME100 Climate List in 2025.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with family members (James Fu, Ivan Lee), social accounts (Instagram, LinkedIn), political stances on sustainability, major achievements from Wikipedia and web search
